questions.question: Write the 4×4 homogeneous transformation matrix for 3D scaling with scale factors sx, sy, sz along the X, Y, and Z axes respectively. Also, explain what happens when one or more scale factors are negative.
keyPoints: Negative scale factors produce reflection (mirroring) about the respective axis
Scale factor of 1 preserves size along that axis
Scale factor of 0 collapses dimensions (projection onto plane)

questions.question: Define what is meant by a composite transformation in 3D computer graphics. Explain why the order of transformations matters when combining multiple transformations into a single matrix.
keyPoints: Composite transformation combines multiple transformations into one matrix through matrix multiplication
Matrix multiplication is generally non-commutative (AB ≠ BA)
Order affects final result: translate-then-rotate differs from rotate-then-translate
Pre-multiplying vs post-multiplying determines whether transformation is in local or world coordinates

questions.question: Derive the 3D rotation matrix for rotation by an angle θ around the Z-axis. Using this matrix, calculate the coordinates of point P(2, 3, 4) after rotating it by 90° around the Z-axis. Show all steps of matrix multiplication.

A 3D object vertex is at position V(1, 1, 1). Perform the following composite transformation in sequence: (a) Translate by (2, 0, 1), (b) Scale by (2, 0.5, 1), (c) Rotate 180° around the X-axis. Find the final transformed vertex position. Also write the composite transformation matrix by multiplying the individual transformation matrices in the correct order.
keyPoints: Composite matrix = R_x × S × T (post-multiplication for local transformations)
Step-by-step: After T: (3,1,2), After S: (6,0.5,2), After R_x: (6,-0.5,-2)
